All right, so we have made it to WayOut Day fulfillment. Okay, so for sales training on WayOut Day, visit Resale or visit Sales, Resale, and then Muir of Change. Okay, so we're not talking about sales training for WayOut Day. We are just talking about how you fulfill it and what needs to happen on WayOut Day. So this is the final day. They are in your program unless they have decided to continue at the halfway meeting because before then, you haven't really had a conversation with them. If they have not decided yet, that is going to be a meeting that you need to have today, so on WayOut Day. So that is going to be a quick 10 to 15 minute meeting. It's not quite as lengthy as the halfway meeting because you've already discussed all of that stuff, right? So outside of that sales meeting, here's what else needs to happen on WayOut Day. You'll need to make sure that you do the following. Review their final results. So see if they reach their goals, right? 20 pounds, 5% body fat loss. See if they're eligible for a prize or a refund or however you do it, but see you need to check the final results. Next, you want to take their after pictures. You have all of their before pictures, so we want to take their after pictures. Then we want to take whiteboard pictures, so they should be taking a picture of them holding up a whiteboard of some kind, saying what kind of results they've gotten during your challenge. Then you want to check to make sure that they wrote a review on Facebook, Google, or Yelp, that is typically part of the contract, that they're writing an honest review of your company. And you can check other things as well, like if they're checking on their Facebook wall and things like that that you have as part of the criteria for your challenge, now would be the time where you check over all of that stuff and make sure that they've complied with it. So then you will need to have the talk about continuing if they are still undecided. So you've done all of the initial stuff, pictures weigh in, checking, all that stuff. It's time to say, hey, let's chat a little bit about your long-term goals and what comes next, right? So you're always long-term mindset and that's a good transition into talking about long-term goals. So after you have that sales meeting, so that past 15 minutes was just everything that you went over into your sales training once you're in that office. Now that that meeting is finished, no matter what the result was, if they're not going to continue, you'll need to see if they qualify for any refund and let them know when they should be expecting it and thank them for completing your challenge. So always set the expectation, let them know when they're going to be getting it, and then leave with some goodwill and thank them for completing it, and hopefully they had an amazing time. Now, if they are going to be continuing, you'll want to give them a what comes next document, okay? So this document should include, and it can just be a piece of paper, it can be a packet, it can be a folder, however you want to package it up, but there's some things that they're going to want to know and it makes sense for them to have a physical copy of it. So membership details, so any details on things they need to know, like, hey, your auto build weekly or whatever it may be, that's a great place for you to stuff it in there so they can review their membership details, right? And EFT, paid in full contract, if you did not give them the contract at the halfway meeting, maybe you are running late, maybe they just had a call and give you confirmation, maybe they said, yeah, no problem, and you just didn't do the contract, this is a perfect place to then give them that contract that they've already agreed to. What comes, what the next steps are for their accountability coach, so if they're staying with you or they're staying with the same accountability coach, let them know that, if they're transitioning to a new accountability coach, let them know who they're transitioning to and when that person's going to reach out to them, could be like an automated message, however it is, let them know who they're next, what the plan is for accountability moving forward, who they're going to be working with, right? What the next step is for the nutrition plan. So typically they won't be following the exact same nutrition plan that they just did over the past six weeks, so what will they be following? Are they getting a customized nutrition plan? Are they going to be following the 42-day detox plan if they did the six-week challenge? Are they going to do the six-week challenge nutrition plan if you signed them up with the 42-day? In the Facebook group, I know that we have a couple post-challenge nutrition plans that you could look up and use, so just basically tell them, hey, this is what our plan is moving forward for nutrition, and your accountability coach is going to go over all that stuff with you, okay? And then anything else that members need to know about, like if you have a VIP Facebook group, if you do anything specific for like member events, this is where you want to let them know about anything else that comes with their membership, that they can sit and review or take a look at. Once they have completed all of this and understand what comes next, whoops, once they've completed all of this and they understand what comes next, then you've successfully completed a challenge with them, okay? So now the goal is to continue providing high-level service and keep them as members as long as you can, okay? And we cover exactly how to do that in the next course, which is called Retention. So we'll see you guys there.
